IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: Our mission is to guide youth toward success through mentorship, education, and community support. We provide holistic, youth-centered programming that includes leadership development through Future Legacies, urban farming and food access education, outdoor exploration through the NextGen Outdoors Initiative, creative and social-emotional learning via Daydreams Club, international cooking classes that build cultural awareness, behavioral health support, and career readiness and youth entrepreneurship education.
IRS990.MissionDesc: Our mission is to guide youth toward success through mentorship, education, and community support. We provide holistic, youth-centered programming that includes leadership development through Future Legacies, urban farming and food access education, outdoor exploration through the NextGen Outdoors Initiative, creative and social-emotional learning via Daydreams Club, international cooking classes that build cultural awareness, behavioral health support, and career readiness and youth entrepreneurship education.
IRS990.Desc: Wisdom in Motions Behavioral Health programming promotes youth mental wellness, resilience, and social-emotional development through a combination of nature-based, creative, and community-centered activities. This program includes the NextGen Outdoors Initiative, Urban Farming education, and Daydreamers Club. Youth participate in outdoor exploration, hands-on food and agriculture learning, and creative social-emotional learning experiences designed to reduce stress, build coping skills, and strengthen connections to peers and trusted adults. These services support overall behavioral health while addressing risk factors associated with juvenile justice involvement.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: Wisdom in Motions Career Readiness and Youth Entrepreneurship program supports youth who are at risk of involvement with the juvenile justice system, as well as youth experiencing housing instability, school disengagement, or other barriers to success. The program provides one-on-one mentoring, workforce readiness training, resume building, interview preparation, and youth entrepreneurship education. Participants develop professional skills, explore career pathways, and receive support in setting and achieving education or employment goals. Programming emphasizes economic self-sufficiency, leadership development, and positive decision-making through consistent mentorship and community partnerships.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: Future Legacies is Wisdom in Motions core youth mentorship program, serving justice-involved and at-risk youth through consistent, relationship-based mentoring. Youth are paired with caring adults who provide guidance, accountability, and support focused on personal growth, leadership development, and long-term goal setting. The program emphasizes positive identity development, decision-making skills, and community connection, helping youth build stability and envision successful futures while reducing risk factors associated with juvenile justice system involvement.

At any time during the calendar year, the organization did not have an interest in, or a signature or other authority over, a financial account in a foreign country (such as a bank account, securities account, or other financial account).

During the year, the organization added new program services including the NextGen Outdoors Initiative, Daydreams Club, international cooking classes, expanded behavioral health support, and enhanced career readiness and youth entrepreneurship programming. These programs further advance the organizations mission of supporting youth development through education, mentorship, and community-based services.
The organization did not have unrelated business taxable income (UBTI) of $1,000 or more during the tax year. Accordingly, Form 990-T was not required to be filed.
The organization did not receive any payments for indoor tanning services during the tax year. As a result, Form 720 was not required to be filed.

At the end of the tax year, the organizations governing body consisted of five voting members. All voting members have equal voting rights, and the governing body did not delegate broad authority to an executive committee or similar committee.
The organization's CEO and Program Director are spouses. The Board is aware of this relationship and follows its conflict of interest procedures for matters involving compensation and governance.
The organization contemporaneously documented meetings and written actions of the governing body through meeting minutes and written records maintained during the tax year.
A draft of Form 990 was provided to members of the governing body prior to filing. Board members were given the opportunity to review and ask questions before submission.

The organization requires annual conflict of interest disclosures from officers, directors, and key employees. Disclosures are reviewed by the Board and any potential conflicts are addressed through recusal and documented review.

The organization uses a compensation review process for the Chief Executive Officer that includes review and approval by the Board of Directors or a committee of the Board composed of individuals without a conflict of interest. The process relies on comparable compensation data from similarly situated nonprofit organizations and documents the basis for compensation decisions contemporaneously.

Compensation for other officers and key employees is determined using a similar process, including review by independent members of the governing body, consideration of comparable compensation data, and contemporaneous documentation of the decision-making process.
The organization makes its Form 990, governing documents, conflict of interest policy, and financial statements available to the public upon request.
N/A Total revenue: 282,901, expenses: 272,793
